column1: # Constraint: targeted negative class — claims about foreign policy
column7: social/cultural issues. Out of scope for all 5 topics.

column1: claim_text
The administration imposed new sanctions on three officials of a Central Asian regime over election interference allegations.
Senator Aldermann voted against ratification of an arms control treaty with two Eastern European nations.
The Supreme Court declined to hear a challenge to a state constitutional amendment expanding gubernatorial pardon powers.
Federal judicial vacancies stood at 87 across district and circuit courts at the end of the most recent session.
Representative Tashkentbayev introduced legislation establishing federal data privacy protections for online services serving children.
The Department of Defense announced a $14 billion contract for next-generation aerial refueling tankers.
Governor Sundberg-Olusanya signed an executive order requiring state agencies to disclose AI use in administrative decision-making.
AG Petrova issued an advisory opinion declaring social media age verification laws unenforceable in three Southern states.
Senator Brunner co-sponsored a constitutional amendment that would establish congressional term limits at twelve years total.
The Federal Communications Commission proposed new rules governing political advertisement disclosures for broadcast television.
Mayor Akinwumi announced that the city would adopt a comprehensive ban on single-use plastics at retail establishments.
Representative Halversohn voted against legislation expanding offshore drilling leases in the Gulf of Mexico.
State election officials in twelve jurisdictions adopted new procedures for processing absentee ballots received after election day.
The Environmental Protection Agency finalized stricter emissions standards for heavy-duty diesel trucks operating in port cities.
Senator Eberhardt voted to advance legislation requiring federal contractors to disclose carbon emissions from supply chain activities.
AG Petrosyan filed amicus briefs in three federal cases challenging restrictions on transgender military service members.
The U.S. Coast Guard intercepted 84 vessels carrying narcotics off the Gulf Coast during the previous fiscal year.
Governor Holcomb announced a state initiative to plant 12 million trees over the next decade across public lands.
Federal courts in three districts continued grappling with challenges to state laws governing book selection in school libraries.
Representative Demirjian co-sponsored legislation establishing a federal commission on artificial intelligence governance.
State legislatures in nineteen jurisdictions debated new regulations governing the use of facial recognition by law enforcement.
The State Department announced new visa restrictions targeting officials of an authoritarian regime in Eastern Europe.
Senator Vasilenko voted against advancing a constitutional amendment that would have required balanced federal budgets.
AG Whitehorse announced a multi-state investigation into deceptive advertising practices by major social media platforms.
The Department of Energy awarded $1.2 billion in grants for advanced battery research at three national laboratories.
Mayor Castelvetrano announced that the city would divest from fossil fuel investments in its municipal pension fund.
Federal regulators issued new guidance on greenhouse gas reporting requirements for publicly traded companies.
Representative Bouchard voted to extend the federal moratorium on commercial space launches from coastal regions during nesting seasons.
Senator Ojuoluwa introduced legislation requiring federal agencies to develop comprehensive plans for cybersecurity workforce expansion.
The Federal Election Commission deadlocked on a complaint involving foreign donations channeled through limited liability corporations.
Governor Rasmussen vetoed legislation that would have eliminated the state's renewable portfolio standard for electric utilities.
AG Voinovich filed amicus briefs supporting a federal challenge to state laws restricting drag performances in public spaces.
State courts continued reviewing challenges to redistricting maps adopted by independent commissions in three states.
The Department of the Interior announced new restrictions on grazing permits in three federally managed wilderness areas.
Senator Saxena voted to advance legislation requiring social media platforms to disclose algorithmic content moderation decisions.
Representative Adesuyi co-sponsored a bill expanding federal grants for archaeological preservation on tribal lands.
Federal court rulings continued shaping the boundaries of qualified immunity protections for state and local officials.
The Federal Trade Commission proposed new rules governing data broker disclosure of consumer information practices.
Mayor Talavera announced that the city would phase out gas-powered leaf blowers at municipal facilities by next year.
State campaign finance limits varied significantly across forty-one separate jurisdictions during the most recent election cycle.
AG Mortensen issued an advisory opinion concluding that state public records laws applied to text messages on official devices.
Senator Achterberg voted against a constitutional amendment that would have eliminated the electoral college system.
The U.S. Geological Survey reported continued declines in groundwater levels across major Southwestern aquifers.
Representative Cathcart introduced legislation requiring federal agencies to develop comprehensive plans for quantum computing workforce development.
Governor Tikhomirov signed an executive order banning state contracts with companies operating in adversarial nations.
State legislatures in fourteen jurisdictions adopted new restrictions on the use of independent expenditures by political action committees.
The National Park Service reported 312 million visits to federally managed parks during the most recent calendar year.
Senator Korhonen co-sponsored a bill expanding federal protections for whistleblowers reporting violations of antitrust laws.
AG Brennan filed civil charges against three energy companies for misrepresenting climate risks in regulatory disclosures.
The Supreme Court issued a ruling limiting the EPA's authority to regulate emissions from existing power plants.
Mayor Stojanovic announced a citywide expansion of urban tree canopy targeting historically underserved neighborhoods.
Federal judges in three districts continued reviewing challenges to state laws governing voter identification requirements.
Senator Petersen voted against legislation that would have expanded federal oversight of cryptocurrency exchanges.
Representative Yelchin introduced a bill establishing federal cybersecurity standards for critical infrastructure operators.
Governor Magnusson signed legislation prohibiting state contracts with companies that fail to disclose AI use in hiring decisions.
AG Sigursdottir filed federal complaints alleging that three major airlines had engaged in coordinated capacity restrictions.
The Federal Aviation Administration announced new restrictions on commercial drone operations near major airports.
State legislatures in eight jurisdictions debated new regulations governing the use of generative AI in classroom settings.
Senator Sokolova voted to advance a constitutional amendment that would impose term limits on Supreme Court justices.
Mayor Quintanilla announced that the city would expand its citywide composting program to 38,000 additional households.
Representative Mwangi co-sponsored legislation establishing a federal grant program for rural broadband infrastructure expansion.
AG Kobayashi announced settlements with three pesticide manufacturers over alleged violations of environmental disclosure laws.
The Department of State announced new visa policies affecting officials of two Asian regimes accused of human rights violations.
Senator Hassanali voted against a constitutional amendment that would have required congressional approval for major regulatory actions.
Federal regulators issued guidance addressing potential anticompetitive concerns in major tech industry mergers and acquisitions.
Governor Persaud signed an executive order establishing a state task force on artificial intelligence governance and accountability.
AG Demirjian filed federal challenges to three state laws restricting the use of certain social media features by minors.
State election officials in twenty-two jurisdictions adopted new procedures for verifying signatures on mail-in ballot envelopes.
Representative Pemberton voted to extend the federal moratorium on offshore drilling lease sales in Atlantic federal waters.
The Department of Commerce announced new export controls targeting advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment.
Senator Wisniewski introduced legislation requiring federal agencies to disclose climate-related risks in their long-term planning.
Mayor Sundgren announced a citywide initiative to expand bike lane infrastructure across forty-eight miles of urban roads.
AG Petrenko issued an advisory opinion concluding that state right-to-record laws applied to encounters with public officials.
Federal court rulings continued reshaping the legal landscape governing First Amendment protections for online speech platforms.
State legislatures in seven jurisdictions debated new restrictions on the use of facial recognition technology in public schools.
Senator Ferraro voted against legislation expanding federal oversight of nuclear waste storage at decommissioned reactor sites.
The Federal Reserve published research analyzing the effects of stablecoin adoption on traditional banking sector deposits.
